stories.headline: US and Israel launch massive military strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei

articles.title: Starmer criticises Trump, saying UK 'does not believe in regime change from skies'
articles.description: The PM told MPs that Trump had expressed his disagreement with the decision not to get involved in the initial strikes.

articles.title: Jeremy Bowen: Three days in, we still have no idea where this war is heading
articles.description: It's hard to predict how the war will end - but those fighting know how they would like it to.

articles.title: What is behind the strategy to take out Iran's leadership?
articles.description: Security analyst Gordon Corera looks at what is likely to happen after much of Iran's most senior leadership is killed.

articles.title: In maps: Strikes across Iran and the Middle East
articles.description: Iranian strikes on targets across the Middle East and Gulf regions continued on Monday after the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran killing the country's supreme leader.

articles.title: Trump defends Iran strikes, offers objectives for military operation
articles.description: His remarks are the first public ones to reporters since the U.S.-Israeli military operation against Iran began Saturday despite weeks of talks designed to stave off a conflict.

articles.title: U.S., Israel's war with Iran widens. And, Texas primaries test Latino support for GOP
articles.description: Israel trades fire with Hezbollah, widening the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran into Lebanon. And, Texas primaries could test if Latino support for the GOP remains strong after 2024's gains.

articles.title: A 4th U.S. soldier has died as the war with Iran further engulfs the region
articles.description: The war over Iran engulfed more of the Middle East and beyond on Monday as strikes intensified, Iran-backed groups stepped up attacks and a fourth U.S. service member was killed in action.

articles.title: Hezbollah strikes Israel as American and Israeli planes pound Iran
articles.description: Iran and Iranian-backed militias fired missiles at Israel and Arab states, apparently hitting the U.S. Embassy compound in Kuwait, while Israel and the United States pounded targets in Iran on Monday.

articles.title: Iran live updates: 6 US service members killed, CENTCOM says
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel launched a joint attack on Iran on Saturday, officials said.

articles.title: WATCH:  3 American fighter jets shot down by friendly fire
articles.description: New video shows American F-15s falling from the sky in Kuwait. The crew members ejecting safely, the U.S. said.

articles.title: WATCH:  Protests, celebrations over US strikes on Iran
articles.description: In the U.S., Iranians and Iranian-Americans are taking to the streets to celebrate and protest the U.S. military strikes and the death of the Iranian supreme leader. ABC News' Jaclyn Lee reports.

articles.title: WATCH:  The latest as the US and Israel attack Iran
articles.description: The Middle East was rocked by another night of air attacks pitting the U.S. and Israel against Iran. ABC News' Chief Foreign Correspondent Ian Pannell reports from Tel Aviv, Israel.

articles.title: WATCH:  What strikes on Iran mean for the Middle East
articles.description: Former U.S. ambassador to NATO retired Lt. Gen. Doug Lute discusses the possible fallout from the joint U.S.-Israel attack.

articles.title: WATCH:  At least 201 people had been killed across Iran: Iranian Red Crescent Society
articles.description: Michael Singh, the managing director and senior fellow at the Washington Institute, joins to examine the U.S. military strikes in the Middle East.

articles.title: Melania Trump presides over U.N. Security Council meeting
articles.description: The White House announced first lady Melania Trump would preside over the meeting last week before the U.S. and Israel launched a joint U.S.-Israeli military mission in Iran.

articles.title: Wagers draw scrutiny after Iran strikes: "Insider trading," senator says
articles.description: Lawmakers are raising concerns that prediction market users are engaging in insider trading to wager on U.S. military actions.

articles.title: Pete Hegseth says Iran military mission will not be "endless"
articles.description: The Pentagon announced Monday that six American service members have been killed in Operation Epic Fury.

articles.title: U.S. death toll in Iran war rises to 6 as Trump warns of long campaign
articles.description: The U.S. military said Monday that the number of Americans killed during the ongoing conflict with Iran now stands at six. Follow live updates on Day 3 of the war.

articles.title: 6 U.S. service members killed in Iranian strike in Kuwait, Pentagon says
articles.description: The first U.S. casualties of the war with Iran occurred among American personnel based in Kuwait.

articles.title: Watch: Rubio takes questions on Iran, comments on alleged strike on school
articles.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke to reporters Monday about the ongoing strikes against Iran. See his remarks.

articles.title: Trump officials likely to be pressed on Iran specifics during congressional briefings this week
articles.description: Trump administration officials will likely face questions about specific details on the military operations in Iran this week when they head to Capitol Hill to brief Congress. CBS News intelligence and national security reporter Olivia Gazis has more.

articles.title: How Trump Decided to Go to War With Iran
articles.description: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was spurred by an Israeli leader determined to end diplomatic negotiations. Few of the president’s advisers voiced opposition.

articles.title: 3 U.S. Planes Are Shot Down in ‘Friendly Fire’ in Kuwait, U.S. Military Says
articles.description: Separately, a drone attack hit the U.S. Embassy compound in the Persian Gulf state, as Iran continues to target American assets across the Middle East.

articles.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.

articles.title: How the Bombing of Iran Is Affecting Lebanon, Kuwait and Other Countries
articles.description: The Middle East is facing deaths and destruction as Iran retaliates against a huge American-Israeli military campaign.

articles.title: 'Whatever it takes': Trump says the Iran conflict will continue
articles.description: President Donald Trump laid out a potential timeline of the Iran conflict saying, "we projected four to five weeks, but we have capability to go far longer than that." The president also said Iran had to be stopped from creating nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles for the safety of U.S. citizens.

articles.title: How Trump decided to strike Iran
articles.description: A last chance to avert war with Iran played out Thursday in Geneva, where Trump's U.S. negotiators told Iran they must not take steps to build a nuclear bomb.

articles.title: Photos: Thousands of Iranians turn out to mourn Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran
articles.description: For many Iranians, the day began with the news they had feared.

articles.title: Trump updates on 'large scale combat operations' in Iran
articles.description: President Trump says the U.S. is continuing to carry out “large scale” operations in Iran to eliminate the “grave threats” that the regime poses to America.

articles.description: Across the United States, Iranian Americans expressed frustration, hope, dread and — above all — concern for relatives still in Iran after the joint U.S.-Israeli attacks.

articles.title: Outside White House, hundreds protest attack on Iran, urge end to conflict
articles.description: The protest near the White House was among the demonstrations that erupted across the country after Israel and the United States launched strikes on Iran.

articles.description: Trump and Netanyahu are in this war for very different reasons. And that will soon show.

articles.description: Vice President JD Vance argued in favor of a larger-scale attack on Iran in the lead-up to Saturday’s strikes on the country, according to the New York Times. Vance reportedly said during a meeting in the White House’s Situation Room on Feb. 18 that launching a limited strike on Iran would be a mistake, arguing…

articles.description: Democrats eyeing a White House bid in 2028 have unanimously opposed President Trump’s strikes on Iran but have split on their rationale, highlighting different approaches on what could be a crucial issue. While many expect the 2028 presidential race to be dominated by domestic economic issues, the differing views offer an early glimpse at how…

Charter,”…

In a lengthy piece that outlined how Trump, at the urging of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, decided to launch military action against Iran, the newspaper reported that Carlson met with the president three…

articles.description: More than half of Americans expect the recent strikes against Iran to grow into a long-term conflict, according to a poll released Monday. In the CNN poll, 56 percent of respondents said they believed “a long-term military conflict between the US and Iran” is “very likely” or “somewhat likely.” Thirty percent in the poll said…

Jeffries and other Democrats have remained unsupportive of the attacks, questioning the military strategy in the region given past tensions and alleging Trump’s actions are…

articles.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) on Sunday lauded U.S. strikes in Iran and said Cuba is “next.” “Cuba’s next. They’re going to follow this communist dictatorship in Cuba. Their days are numbered,” Graham said during an appearance on Fox News’s “Sunday Night in America.” “The Iranian regime, the mother ship of international terrorism is about to…

articles.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the US attacked Iran because they knew Israel was going to launch a military strike, and the Trump administration worried about retaliation from Tehran. “We knew that there was going to be an Israeli action,” he told reporters on Capitol Hill after briefing lawmakers. “We knew that that would...

articles.title: Who is running Iran now?
articles.description: Iranian authorities have announced a three-member interim council to run the government amid the unprecedented joint U.S.-Israeli operations that took out both Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and several other senior officials. The three council members will be Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian; the chief justice of the Supreme Court, Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Ejei; and a member of the […]

Starmer, speaking before the British Parliament on Monday, mentioned the U.S. leader by name as he outlined his reasoning for withholding U.K. military personnel from “offensive strikes” — primarily, […]

Various prominent Democrats, including 2028 hopefuls such as former Vice President Kamala Harris, Gov. Gavin Newsom (D-CA), and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-NY), have vocally opposed […]

analysis.summary: The United States and Israel launched joint military strikes on Iran beginning Saturday (dubbed 'Operation Epic Fury'), killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other senior officials. Iran retaliated with missile strikes across the Middle East, hitting targets in Israel, Kuwait, and other Gulf states, while the U.S. death toll rose to six service members. President Trump projected the conflict lasting 4-5 weeks, as the war widened to involve Hezbollah in Lebanon and sparked global protests, diplomatic condemnation, and sharp domestic political divisions.
agreements: The US and Israel launched joint military strikes on Iran beginning Saturday, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
The military operation is called 'Operation Epic Fury'
Six US service members have been killed in the conflict as of Monday
Trump projected the conflict could last 4-5 weeks but said it could go longer
Iran retaliated with missile strikes on Israel and US assets across the Middle East, including hitting the US Embassy compound in Kuwait
Three US fighter jets were shot down by friendly fire in Kuwait, with crews ejecting safely
Hezbollah joined the conflict by striking Israel
Secretary of State Rubio said the US knew Israel was going to strike Iran and acted to protect American interests
Spain refused to allow the US to use its air bases for Iran operations
UK Prime Minister Starmer refused to participate in offensive strikes against Iran, drawing criticism from Trump
Russia and Turkey condemned the strikes, while NATO praised the action but said the alliance would not be involved
Democrats largely opposed the strikes, though with varying rationales
Iran established a three-member interim governing council following Khamenei's death

disagreements.topic: Framing of the strikes' legitimacy and motivation

positions.position: Frame strikes as justified, necessary response to Iran's nuclear ambitions and threat; National Review states 'Iran Chose the Hard Way' and characterizes Khamenei as a 'despot'
Frames strikes as potentially illegal aggression, emphasizes strikes hitting housing, hospitals, and police stations in Tehran, and warns the war might push Iran toward a nuclear bomb faster
UK PM Starmer frames the action as 'regime change from skies' that the UK does not believe in, questioning the strategy's legitimacy

disagreements.topic: Whether the US was independently motivated or drawn in by Israel

positions.position: Reports Trump's decision was 'spurred by an Israeli leader determined to end diplomatic negotiations' with few advisers voicing opposition; presents Netanyahu as the driving force
Reports Rubio 'suggests US strikes on Iran were influenced by Israeli plans,' framing the US as following Israel's lead
Rubio framed the US action as protecting American troops from an 'imminent threat' after learning of Israel's plans, portraying US as acting independently for its own security
Reports last-chance negotiations in Geneva failed before strikes, emphasizing the diplomatic process preceded military action

disagreements.topic: UK's level of involvement in the conflict

positions.position: Reports 'Starmer lets US use bases for Iran clash,' highlighting a drone strike at a British base in Cyprus and describing a 'military, legal quagmire'
Report Starmer refused to join offensive campaigns and criticized Trump, with the UK 'not believing in regime change from skies'

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on civilian casualties and humanitarian impact

positions.position: Leads with strikes hitting housing, hospitals, and civilian infrastructure in Tehran; reports protests in India against Khamenei's killing
Reports at least 201 people killed across Iran per Iranian Red Crescent Society
Focus primarily on military targets, regime leadership elimination, and strategic objectives with minimal emphasis on civilian casualties

disagreements.topic: American public opinion on the strikes

positions.position: Reports 'majority of Americans oppose Trump's Iran strikes' based on multiple polls, and that many of Trump's own voters didn't want the attack
Reports 56% expect the strikes to grow into long-term conflict but does not characterize overall opposition as dominant
Emphasize that Democrats opposing the strikes risk looking 'foolish' if the mission succeeds, framing opposition as politically motivated rather than reflecting popular sentiment

disagreements.topic: Internal White House deliberations and dissent

positions.position: Reports VP Vance pushed for a larger-scale attack, and Tucker Carlson met with Trump three times trying to talk him out of going to war
Do not mention internal dissent from Carlson or debate within Trump's circle; focus on unified administration messaging

uniqueAngles.angle: Reports CIA shared Khamenei's location for the Israeli strike; claims Trump referenced Iran's two assassination attempts against him ('I Got Khamenei First, Tried to Kill Me Twice'); reports former President Ahmadinejad also killed in strikes
Provides specific details on Iran's interim governing council: President Masoud Pezeshkian, Chief Justice Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Ejei, and a third member of the Assembly of Experts

Highlights Sen. Fetterman breaking with Democrats to defend the strikes, calling regime leaders 'garbage'; identifies hardline cleric Alireza Arafi as potential Khamenei successor; reports fires at Bandar Abbas naval HQ and Strait of Hormuz traffic stalled

Reports Sen. Lindsey Graham said 'Cuba is next' after the Iran strikes; covers Speaker Johnson expecting a War Powers vote on Thursday; details splits among 2028 Democratic presidential hopefuls on their specific rationales for opposing strikes
Reports on prediction market wagers drawing scrutiny with a senator calling it 'insider trading'; covers Melania Trump presiding over a UN Security Council meeting on the conflict
Features photos of thousands of Iranians mourning Khamenei in Tehran, providing ground-level perspective from inside Iran

Reports on protests across India against Khamenei's killing; features former US diplomat warning the war 'might push Iran to a bomb faster'; provides detailed reporting on civilian infrastructure damage including hospitals
Provides detailed behind-the-scenes account of Trump's decision-making process, reporting that Netanyahu was the driving force and that few advisers voiced opposition
Focuses on the emotional and personal experiences of the Iranian American diaspora, documenting the 'mix of joy, unease' and concern for relatives still in Iran

Analyzes how the strikes could reshape Democratic primary dynamics, with AIPAC money and intervention becoming new litmus tests; reports Trump's own base was not overwhelmingly supportive before the strikes

Reports on Iran's soccer federation casting doubt on World Cup participation in the US; covers the emergency UN Security Council meeting where the UN chief condemned the attacks; explains Iran's succession process
Jeremy Bowen analysis notes that three days in, the outcome and direction of the war remain deeply uncertain

Frames the U.S.-Israeli air partnership as a milestone with 'aircraft flying as equals,' portraying Israel's emergence as the Middle East's preeminent military power; debates the wisdom of authorizing Khamenei's assassination through historical parallels

stories.headline: Oil and energy prices surge as Iran conflict disrupts Strait of Hormuz and global markets

articles.title: Oil prices surge amid fears over Iran war
articles.description: Global oil prices are in the high $70s as traffic through Strait of Hormuz comes to a halt. Some analysts have warned they could top $100 a barrel if the stoppage is prolonged.

articles.title: Stocks close essentially flat in 1st trading day after Iran attack
articles.description: Oil prices spiked more than 7% on Monday.

articles.title: Prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure would cause oil prices to surge, experts warn
articles.description: The Iran war is renewing concerns about the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. A prolonged closure could sharply drive up oil prices, experts said.

articles.title: Iran strikes: Breaking down the impact so far on oil prices
articles.description: Oil prices have climbed amid the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran. Axios national correspondent Emily Peck joins CBS News to discuss.

articles.title: Higher gas prices are likely coming to the pump after oil prices jump in wake of U.S. strikes in Iran
articles.description: The price of oil surged Monday as President Donald Trump signaled that the conflict between the U.S., Israel and Iran would potentially be drawn out for longer than a few weeks, raising concerns that the global energy market would face disruptions as a result.U.S.

articles.title: Why QatarEnergy’s LNG production halt could shake up global gas markets
articles.description: Qatar supplies 20 percent of the world's LNG. If that's off the table, countries must scramble for the remaining gas.

articles.title: As global markets tanked over Iran, U.S. stocks were mostly unscathed. Here’s why.
articles.description: There is an old saying in markets: Buy when the bullets (or bombs or missiles) fly.

articles.title: Gold just passed its stress test — what happens next will tell investors if they’re right
articles.description: Gold did exactly what it was supposed to after the Iran strike. Is the spike temporary or a new era for the safe-haven trade?

articles.title: Iran conflict unlikely to hurt U.S. economy or boost inflation — but the Fed won’t be quick to cut rates
articles.description: The U.S. attack on Iran won’t boost U.S. inflation or harm the economy in a major way, analysts say, unless in the unlikely case that the conflict drags on for months and sharply raises the price of oil.

articles.title: Trump’s attacks on Iran could make many things more expensive, despite his affordability promises
articles.description: How do you campaign on “affordability” if you just pushed up prices?

articles.title: Here is the ‘worst-case scenario’ for oil and the Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: Oil prices settled at their highest levels since June on Monday, as the conflict in the Middle East brings with it an inherent risk to the world’s available crude supplies.

articles.title: Bonds log biggest selloff in 9 months as Iran conflict sparks unusual Treasury moves
articles.description: Mortgage rates and more will be vulnerable to the surge in the 10-year Treasury yield.

articles.title: Iran Moves to Close Strait of Hormuz, World Faces Global Oil Shock - Newsmax
articles.description: Iran Moves to Close Strait of Hormuz, World Faces Global Oil Shock  Newsmax

analysis.summary: U.S.-Israeli military strikes on Iran have disrupted traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, causing oil prices to surge over 7% with prices reaching the high $70s per barrel and U.S. gasoline crossing $3 per gallon. Experts warn that a prolonged closure of the strait could push oil prices above $100 per barrel, while the broader impact on global energy supply chains—including Qatar's LNG production—has rattled markets worldwide.
agreements: Oil prices surged significantly following U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran
The Strait of Hormuz is a central strategic concern due to disruptions in shipping traffic
Experts warn that a prolonged conflict or strait closure could drive prices much higher
U.S. gasoline prices are rising as a result of the conflict
Global energy supply is being disrupted by both tanker disruptions and facility shutdowns
U.S. stock markets were relatively resilient compared to global markets on the first trading day after the attacks

disagreements.topic: Who is responsible for Strait of Hormuz disruption

positions.position: Frames Iran as actively moving to close the Strait of Hormuz ('Iran Moves to Close Strait of Hormuz')
Describes traffic through the Strait as coming to a halt without attributing agency to Iran specifically
Frames disruptions as resulting from 'attacks in Middle East' generally, including tanker disruptions and facility shutdowns

disagreements.topic: Likely economic impact on the U.S. economy

positions.position: One analysis says the conflict is 'unlikely to hurt U.S. economy or boost inflation' unless it drags on for months and sharply raises oil prices
A separate article argues Trump's attacks on Iran 'could make many things more expensive,' contradicting his affordability promises
Frames the Iran war as posing a 'new risk to US economic resilience,' suggesting meaningful economic concern
Emphasizes Trump signaled the conflict could last longer than a few weeks, raising concerns about sustained energy market disruptions

disagreements.topic: Severity of the oil price spike

positions.position: Reports prices in the high $70s and warns they could top $100 a barrel if the stoppage is prolonged
Reports oil prices spiked more than 7% on Monday, with stocks closing essentially flat
Notes oil settled at highest levels since June and explores 'worst-case scenario' for the Strait of Hormuz

disagreements.topic: Political framing of the conflict's economic consequences

positions.position: Highlights the political tension between Trump's affordability campaign promises and the price increases caused by his Iran policy
Notes Trump signaled the conflict would be drawn out, framing him as contributing to market uncertainty
Frames the story around Iran's actions causing a 'global oil shock,' placing responsibility on Iran rather than U.S. policy

uniqueAngles.angle: Uniquely focuses on Qatar's LNG production halt and its outsized impact on global gas markets, noting Qatar supplies 20% of the world's LNG—a dimension of the energy crisis largely absent from other coverage
Reports a specific incident of a fuel tanker ablaze in the Strait of Hormuz after a drone strike, attributed to Iran's Revolutionary Guards
Provides the specific benchmark that U.S. gasoline has crossed the $3 per gallon mark, framing it as a political test for Trump's Iran war
Covers the bond market reaction, noting the biggest Treasury selloff in 9 months and implications for mortgage rates
Analyzes gold's performance as a safe-haven asset during the crisis, asking whether the spike is temporary or signals a new era
Explains why U.S. stocks were mostly unscathed even as global markets tanked, invoking the old market adage to 'buy when the bullets fly'
Specifically attributes part of the oil price surge to Trump signaling the conflict could last longer than a few weeks, directly tying presidential rhetoric to market movement
Distinguishes between current price impacts and the potential for much sharper increases, centering expert warnings about a prolonged Strait closure scenario

stories.headline: House Oversight Committee releases video of Bill and Hillary Clinton depositions in Epstein probe

articles.title: Video of Clinton depositions in Epstein investigation released by House Republicans
articles.description: Over hours of testimony, the Clintons both denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes prior to his pleading guilty in 2008 to state charges in Florida for soliciting prostitution from an underage girl.

articles.title: Video of Clintons' depositions in House Epstein probe is released
articles.description: Video from the depositions of ex-President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton in the House Oversight panel's Epstein probe was released Monday.

articles.title: Video of Clintons' Epstein testimony released by House committee
articles.description: The House Oversight Committee released recordings of last week's depositions with former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.

articles.title: Videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton's Epstein testimony released by House panel
articles.description: he House Oversight Committee on Monday released video of last week's depositions with former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton as part of its investigation into convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has more.

articles.title: House Oversight Committee releases videos of Clinton depositions on Jeffrey Epstein
articles.description: The House Oversight Committee released video of last week’s interviews with former President Bill Clinton and Secretary Hillary Clinton in its Jeffrey Epstein probe.

articles.title: Hillary Clinton Epstein Deposition: Watch Full Testimony
articles.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton frequently referred many of the committee's questions to her husband, Bill Clinton.

articles.title: Bill Clinton Epstein Deposition: Watch Full Testimony
articles.description: This is the first time a former president has been compelled to testify before Congress.

articles.title: House panel releases videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton depositions
articles.description: The House Oversight Committee on Monday released video footage of its depositions of Bill and Hillary Clinton, showing the former president and former secretary of state’s responses as they were grilled about their connections to deceased sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Hillary Clinton, during her Thursday appearance, told lawmakers she did not recall ever meeting Epstein, and…

articles.title: WATCH: Hillary Clinton storms out of Epstein deposition after House lawmaker leaks photo from inside
articles.description: 'I am done with this,' Clinton said before leaving the room

articles.title: WATCH: House releases Bill and Hillary Clinton’s Epstein deposition videos
articles.description: The House Committee on Oversight and Reform released videos of former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton‘s testimonies on Monday following last week’s hours-long depositions related to lawmakers’ extensive Jeffrey Epstein investigation. The videos of Hillary and Bill, which totaled more than nine hours in combined total length, were taken last […]

articles.title: The Epstein Panic
articles.description: There’s a reason that it is not the usual practice of the DOJ to release unverified, disparaging information about people it’s not charging with crimes.

analysis.summary: The House Oversight Committee released videos on Monday of depositions of former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton conducted the previous week as part of the committee's investigation into convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Both Clintons denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes prior to his 2008 guilty plea, and the combined testimony totaled more than nine hours.
agreements: The House Oversight Committee released video of the Clinton depositions on Monday
Both Bill and Hillary Clinton were deposed as part of the committee's Jeffrey Epstein investigation
The depositions took place the week before the videos were released
Jeffrey Epstein was a convicted sex offender who is now deceased

disagreements.topic: Framing of Hillary Clinton's deposition conduct

positions.position: Leads with Hillary Clinton 'storming out' of the deposition after a House lawmaker leaked a photo from inside, quoting her saying 'I am done with this'
Neutrally describes Hillary Clinton telling lawmakers she did not recall meeting Epstein, with no mention of her walking out
Presents the video release straightforwardly without highlighting any dramatic moments
Presents the video release straightforwardly without highlighting any dramatic moments

disagreements.topic: Overall framing of the investigation's legitimacy and purpose

positions.position: Raises concerns about releasing unverified, disparaging information about people not being charged with crimes, questioning the practice itself
Presents the release as a straightforward outcome of lawmakers' 'extensive' Epstein investigation without questioning its propriety
Frames the story around the dramatic confrontation, implying the Clintons had something to hide or were uncooperative

uniqueAngles.angle: Reports this was the first time a former president has been compelled to testify before Congress
Notes that Hillary Clinton frequently referred the committee's questions to her husband Bill Clinton
Exclusively focuses on Hillary Clinton storming out of the deposition after a lawmaker leaked a photo from inside the room
Specifies the combined deposition videos totaled more than nine hours
Specifies that both Clintons denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes prior to his 2008 guilty plea in Florida for soliciting prostitution from an underage girl
Reports that Hillary Clinton told lawmakers she did not recall ever meeting Epstein

Takes an opinion/analysis approach questioning whether DOJ and congressional investigations should release disparaging information about uncharged individuals, framing the broader probe as an 'Epstein Panic'

stories.headline: Travelers stranded and flights disrupted across Middle East amid Iran conflict

articles.title: 'Everything has changed': Missile attacks shatter Dubai's safe haven image
articles.description: Dubai’s reputation as a prosperous destination for expats and influencers has changed for some following Iranian strikes.

articles.title: Some Middle East flights resume, but thousands of travelers are still stranded by war
articles.description: Limited flights out of the Middle East resumed on Monday. But hundreds of thousands of travelers are still stranded in the region after attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel.

articles.title: WATCH:  Americans scramble to leave Middle East amid strikes
articles.description: The fighting in the region is leaving thousands of Americans there uncertain about their situation. ABC News' Chief International Correspondent James Longman reports from Turkey.

articles.title: They flew to Dubai for a romantic anniversary getaway. Then the bombing started.
articles.description: An American couple who flew to Dubai have been stranded after Iran retaliated to attacks by the U.S. and Israel by striking its neighbors like the UAE.

articles.title: UAE airlines to resume limited number of flights, mainly for repatriations - Reuters
articles.description: UAE airlines to resume limited number of flights, mainly for repatriations  Reuters

articles.title: Stranded travelers scramble to make new connections as war shuts much of Middle East to air travel - AP News
articles.description: Stranded travelers scramble to make new connections as war shuts much of Middle East to air travel  AP News

analysis.summary: Military conflict involving Iran, the U.S., and Israel has led to widespread flight disruptions across the Middle East, stranding hundreds of thousands of travelers. UAE airlines have begun resuming limited flights, mainly for repatriation purposes, but most travelers remain unable to leave the region. The crisis has particularly affected Dubai, which was struck by Iranian retaliatory attacks, shattering its image as a safe haven.
agreements: Flights across the Middle East have been severely disrupted due to the Iran conflict
Large numbers of travelers, including Americans, are stranded in the region
Dubai and the UAE have been directly affected by the military strikes
Some limited flights have begun to resume, though the situation remains dire for most stranded travelers
Iran conducted retaliatory strikes against neighboring countries including the UAE

disagreements.topic: Scale of stranded travelers

positions.position: Reports 'hundreds of thousands' of travelers are still stranded
References 'thousands of Americans' uncertain about their situation
Describes 'stranded travelers' without specifying a number

disagreements.topic: Framing of who initiated the conflict

positions.position: Frames the situation as resulting from 'attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel,' placing U.S./Israel actions first
Frames it as 'Iran retaliated to attacks by the U.S. and Israel,' emphasizing Iran's strikes as retaliatory
References 'Iranian strikes' without explicitly detailing the sequence of escalation

disagreements.topic: Focus and framing of the crisis

positions.position: Frames the story around Dubai's shattered reputation as a safe, prosperous destination
Frames the story primarily around American citizens and their uncertainty
Frames the story around the practical logistics of travelers making new connections
Frames the story through a personal human-interest narrative of a stranded couple

uniqueAngles.angle: Uniquely examines the broader reputational damage to Dubai as a destination for expats and influencers, suggesting a lasting shift in perception

Reports specifically from Turkey, suggesting it has become a transit or gathering point for Americans leaving the region, with on-the-ground reporting by Chief International Correspondent James Longman
Uses a specific human-interest story of an American couple on a romantic anniversary trip to Dubai to personalize the crisis

Focuses narrowly on the operational detail that UAE airlines are resuming flights primarily for repatriation purposes, suggesting a structured evacuation-style approach rather than normal commercial service
Provides the most specific estimate of the scale of disruption, citing 'hundreds of thousands' of stranded travelers

stories.headline: Texas Senate primary becomes most expensive in history as candidates battle in heated races

articles.title: Texas primaries could test whether Latino support for GOP is holding after 2024 gains
articles.description: The GOP made big strides with Latino voters in 2024. Immigration and the economy were key drivers, but two years later, those issues could sway them to back Democrats in primaries happening this week.

articles.title: Texas Senate Primary Becomes Most Expensive in History
articles.description: The primary race for Senate in Texas has become the most expensive on record, with John Cornyn and James Talarico heavily outspending their rivals. But Mr. Cornyn seems to be in trouble.

articles.title: Bitter Texas Senate race forces Dems to confront racial divisions
articles.description: Jasmine Crockett and James Talarico have been locked in a fierce Senate primary loaded with racial overtones and questions about electability.

articles.title: Kamala Harris backs Jasmine Crockett in bitter Texas Democratic Senate primary
articles.description: The former vice president recorded a robocall to boost Crockett over James Talarico.

articles.title: Bannon blasts Trump campaign aides in Texas Senate showdown
articles.description: Steve Bannon has gone all-in for Ken Paxton in Texas. But two of Trump's top campaign hands are backing John Cornyn — opening up a rift on the right.

articles.title: Sen. Thune Warns: GOP Risk Losing Texas Senate Seat - Newsmax
articles.description: Sen. Thune Warns: GOP Risk Losing Texas Senate Seat  Newsmax

analysis.summary: The Texas Senate primaries have become the most expensive in history, with fierce contests on both sides. The Republican primary features John Cornyn facing a challenge from Ken Paxton amid intra-party divisions, while the Democratic primary pits Jasmine Crockett against James Talarico in a race marked by racial tensions and high-profile endorsements. Multiple sources suggest the GOP seat could be vulnerable.
agreements: The Texas Senate primaries are historically competitive and expensive
John Cornyn faces a serious challenge in the Republican primary
James Talarico is a major contender in the Democratic Senate primary
Jasmine Crockett and James Talarico are the leading Democratic primary candidates
Both party primaries have significant internal divisions

disagreements.topic: Primary framing and emphasis: Democratic vs. Republican side

positions.position: Emphasizes record-breaking spending across the race and frames Cornyn as being in trouble
Gives significant attention to both sides but highlights racial divisions and endorsement battles in the Democratic primary, and the Bannon vs. Trump aides rift on the Republican side
Frames the primaries primarily through the lens of Latino voter realignment and whether 2024 GOP gains will hold
Frames the story around the risk of Republicans losing the Texas Senate seat entirely, citing Senate leader Thune's warning

disagreements.topic: Nature of the Democratic primary contest

positions.position: Characterizes the Crockett-Talarico race as loaded with racial overtones and questions about electability
Focuses on Talarico's heavy spending as the defining feature of his candidacy rather than racial dynamics

uniqueAngles.angle: Examines whether Latino voters who shifted to the GOP in 2024 over immigration and the economy might swing back to Democrats, using the Texas primaries as a test case
Reports that Kamala Harris recorded a robocall endorsing Jasmine Crockett over Talarico in the Democratic primary
Details an intra-MAGA rift where Steve Bannon backs Ken Paxton while two top Trump campaign aides support Cornyn
Highlights Senate Majority Leader Thune explicitly warning that the GOP risks losing the Texas Senate seat
Highlights racial dynamics and tensions within the Democratic coalition as a central theme of the Crockett-Talarico primary

stories.headline: Rep. Ryan Zinke announces retirement from Congress amid growing wave of GOP departures

articles.title: Montana Rep. Ryan Zinke won't seek reelection, becoming latest GOP retirement
articles.description: Rep. Ryan Zinke of Montana announced Monday he will not run for reelection, becoming the latest Republican to retire.

articles.title: Republican Ryan Zinke Leaving Congress: What We Know
articles.description: Republicans currently hold a razor thin House majority under Mike Johnson ahead of midterm elections in November.

articles.title: Zinke announces he won’t seek reelection
articles.description: Rep. Ryan Zinke (R-Mont.), a former Interior Secretary during President Trump’s first term, said Monday he will not seek reelection this November. Zinke, who has represented Montana in the lower chamber for three non-consecutive terms, wrote in a letter to Montanans that he has “quietly undergone” multiple surgeries since returning to Congress in January 2023…

articles.title: Trump Cabinet alum Ryan Zinke joins mass exodus of lawmakers leaving Congress
articles.description: GOP Rep. Ryan Zinke announces he will not seek re-election in 2026, citing health issues from his Navy SEAL career as the House Republican exodus continues.

articles.title: Montana Republican Ryan Zinke to retire and join exodus from House
articles.description: Rep. Ryan Zinke (R-MT) is retiring after this year and will not seek another term in the House of Representatives, joining a mass exodus of Republicans opting out of reelection to seek another office or leave politics altogether. Zinke has represented Montana’s 1st Congressional District since 2023, previously serving as interior secretary during President Donald […]

articles.title: Here’s where House and Senate retirements stand in 2026
articles.description: Nearly 11% of lawmakers are looking to make a change after the 2026 midterm elections, with several in both chambers either retiring or seeking higher office. The House received its first departure on Jan. 5. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-GA) announced in November that she would retire at the beginning of the year and not […]

analysis.summary: Rep. Ryan Zinke (R-MT), a former Interior Secretary under President Trump, announced Monday that he will not seek reelection in 2026, citing health issues stemming from his Navy SEAL career. His departure adds to a growing wave of Republican retirements from the House, where the GOP holds a slim majority ahead of the 2026 midterm elections.
agreements: Ryan Zinke announced Monday he will not seek reelection
Zinke is a Republican representing Montana in the U.S. House
Zinke previously served as Interior Secretary during Trump's first term
His retirement is part of a broader wave of Republican departures from Congress
Health issues related to his military service were cited as a reason for his decision

disagreements.topic: Framing of the broader GOP departure trend

positions.position: Describes it as a 'mass exodus of lawmakers leaving Congress'
Describes it as a 'mass exodus of Republicans' and notes nearly 11% of all lawmakers are looking to make a change after 2026
Uses more restrained language, calling Zinke 'the latest Republican to retire'
Focuses on the political stakes, emphasizing Republicans' 'razor thin House majority' ahead of midterms

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on Zinke's identity and background

positions.position: Leads with Zinke as a 'Trump Cabinet alum' and emphasizes his Navy SEAL career
Leads with his role as 'former Interior Secretary' and emphasizes his health struggles and multiple surgeries
Identifies him simply as 'Rep. Ryan Zinke of Montana'

uniqueAngles.angle: Provides specific detail that Zinke has served three non-consecutive terms in the House and wrote in a letter that he has 'quietly undergone' multiple surgeries since returning to Congress in January 2023

The second article provides broader context on the full scope of 2026 retirements across both chambers, noting that Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene was the first House departure, announced in November and effective January 5
Focuses on the political implications for House Speaker Mike Johnson and the razor-thin Republican majority heading into midterms
Specifies Zinke represents Montana's 1st Congressional District and notes some retiring members are leaving to seek higher office rather than leave politics altogether
Specifically highlights Zinke's Navy SEAL career as the source of his health issues in the headline itself

stories.headline: France announces expansion of nuclear deterrence to European allies

articles.title: France to boost nuclear arsenal and extend deterrence to European allies
articles.description: Emmanuel Macron said eight countries could enjoy protection from France's nuclear umbrella - but that Paris would retain sole decision-making power.

articles.title: France to increase nuclear warheads, lend nuclear aircraft to Europe allies
articles.description: Macron's 'advanced deterrence' will be a nuclear-security relationship with key allies distinct from NATO arrangements.

articles.title: Denmark to cooperate with France on nuclear deterrence - Reuters
articles.description: Denmark to cooperate with France on nuclear deterrence  Reuters

articles.title: France to boost nuclear arsenal, involve European allies in deterrence - Reuters
articles.description: France to boost nuclear arsenal, involve European allies in deterrence  Reuters

analysis.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron announced plans to expand France's nuclear arsenal and extend nuclear deterrence protections to European allies in a new security arrangement. The initiative would involve multiple European countries in France's nuclear umbrella while Paris retains sole authority over nuclear decision-making, marking a significant shift in European defense posture distinct from existing NATO structures.
agreements: France plans to boost its nuclear arsenal
European allies will be involved in or benefit from France's nuclear deterrence
President Macron is the driving force behind the announcement

disagreements.topic: Framing of the initiative's relationship to NATO

positions.position: Explicitly frames the arrangement as 'distinct from NATO arrangements,' emphasizing it as an alternative security structure
Does not reference NATO, focusing instead on France's sole decision-making power
Uses broader language of 'involving European allies' without specifying the NATO relationship

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on what France is offering allies

positions.position: Highlights concrete military assets being shared, specifically the lending of nuclear-capable aircraft to allies
Emphasizes the protective 'umbrella' concept and the constraint that France retains sole decision-making power
Frames it more generally as 'involving' allies in deterrence and as bilateral cooperation

uniqueAngles.angle: Specifies that eight countries could be covered under France's nuclear umbrella and stresses that Paris retains sole decision-making power
Uniquely highlights the lending of nuclear-capable aircraft to European allies and uses the term 'advanced deterrence' to describe the arrangement, framing it as an increase in warheads
Specifically identifies Denmark as a country cooperating with France on nuclear deterrence, providing a concrete bilateral example of the broader initiative

stories.headline: Supreme Court appears sympathetic to loosening ban on gun ownership for marijuana users

articles.title: Supreme Court seems open to loosening law barring marijuana users from owning guns
articles.description: The Supreme Court seemed likely Monday to loosen a federal law that bars marijuana users from owning guns in a case that crossed typical political lines.

articles.title: A Supreme Court case over whether marijuana users can own guns is creating unusual alliances - AP News
articles.description: A Supreme Court case over whether marijuana users can own guns is creating unusual alliances  AP News

articles.title: Founding-era drunkards dominate at Supreme Court gun argument
articles.description: The Supreme Court justices tripped through the world of controlled substances and alcohol on Monday as they scrutinized whether the government can constitutionally bar unlawful drug users from possessing firearms.   The arguments revealed a majority sympathetic to a marijuana user challenging his criminal prosecution under the Second Amendment, though the contours of the court’s ruling…

articles.title: Gorsuch questions how one regular marijuana gummy could allow for taking away gun rights
articles.description: Justice Neil Gorsuch sharply questioned the Justice Department over the amount of regular unlawful drug usage necessary to trigger a federal gun charge on Monday during arguments in a key Second Amendment case for drug users. The Supreme Court heard arguments in the case United States v. Hemani, where Ali Hemani challenged the constitutionality of […]

analysis.summary: The Supreme Court heard oral arguments in a case challenging the federal law that bars unlawful drug users from possessing firearms, with a majority of justices appearing sympathetic to the marijuana user challenging the ban. The case has created unusual political alliances, crossing typical ideological lines on both gun rights and drug policy.
agreements: The Supreme Court heard oral arguments on Monday in a case involving whether marijuana users can be barred from owning guns
A majority of justices appeared sympathetic to loosening or striking down the federal prohibition
The case has created unusual or cross-ideological political alliances

disagreements.topic: Framing and emphasis of the case

positions.position: Emphasizes the historical analysis angle, focusing on founding-era comparisons to alcohol and drunkards as central to the argument
Frames the story primarily around Justice Gorsuch's sharp questioning of the Justice Department, focusing on the threshold of drug use needed to trigger a gun charge
Frames the story around the unusual political alliances the case is creating
Takes a broader framing that the court seems likely to loosen the federal law

uniqueAngles.angle: Specifically identifies the case as United States v. Hemani and names the challenger as Ali Hemani, and highlights Gorsuch's specific questioning about whether consuming a single marijuana gummy could justify taking away gun rights
Focuses on the historical analysis involving founding-era attitudes toward alcohol and drunkards as a key element of the oral arguments
Emphasizes the unusual cross-partisan alliances formed around this case
Notes the case crossed typical political lines, suggesting bipartisan interest in the outcome

stories.headline: Pentagon's use of Anthropic AI in Iran strikes sparks controversy

articles.description: Axios senior AI reporter Madison Mills breaks down how Anthropic’s Claude AI was used for intelligence analysis and targeting in Operation Epic Fury.

articles.title: How the US hit Iran with a banned AI

articles.title: What to know about the clash between the Pentagon and Anthropic over military’s AI use - AP News
articles.description: What to know about the clash between the Pentagon and Anthropic over military’s AI use  AP News

articles.title: On Anthropic, Trump Administration Revives Bill of Attainder–Style Extortion
articles.description: This is thuggish behavior.

analysis.summary: The Pentagon used Anthropic's Claude AI for intelligence analysis and targeting during military strikes on Iran in an operation called 'Epic Fury,' sparking a dispute between the Defense Department and Anthropic over the permissibility of such military applications. The controversy expanded to include debate over the Trump administration's response to Anthropic's objections.
agreements: Anthropic's AI technology was used by the US military in connection with strikes on Iran
A significant dispute exists between the Pentagon and Anthropic over the military's use of the AI
The use of AI in this military context is controversial and raises policy questions

disagreements.topic: Framing of the AI's use in military operations

positions.position: Frames the story around how AI 'supported' US targeting, using neutral, technical language about intelligence analysis
Frames the AI as 'banned' — emphasizing that the military use violated Anthropic's own policies, implying the US used the AI improperly

Takes an explainer approach ('What to know'), positioning the story as a factual overview of the Pentagon-Anthropic clash for a general audience

stories.headline: FBI investigates Austin bar shooting that killed 2 and wounded 14 as possible terrorism

articles.title: Police release names of victims in Austin bar shooting

articles.description: FBI investigates Texas bar shooting that killed 2 and wounded 14 as possible terrorist act  AP News

articles.description: Nancy was reported missing from her Arizona home on Feb. 1.

stories.headline: US measles response: CDC urges vaccines as meeting on elimination status delayed

articles.title: As Kennedy Turns From Vaccines, MAHA Allies See a ‘Betrayal’
articles.description: As the midterm elections approach, the health secretary is focusing on “real food.” Anger is rising among some of his supporters.

articles.title: Meeting on U.S. Measles Status Is Delayed Until November
articles.description: The decision to revoke the country’s elimination status would now likely come after the U.S. midterm elections, experts noted.

articles.title: Acting CDC director Bhattacharya urges measles vaccines
articles.description: Jay Bhattacharya, the recently appointed acting director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), on Monday urged families to consider vaccinating against measles as outbreaks across the country continue. In a video statement posted on the social media platform X, Bhattacharya discussed the steps that the CDC is taking the address the measles…

analysis.summary: As measles outbreaks continue across the US, acting CDC director Jay Bhattacharya has urged families to vaccinate against measles. Meanwhile, a key international meeting to evaluate the US measles elimination status has been delayed until November, pushing any decision past the US midterm elections. Health Secretary Kennedy's pivot away from vaccine issues toward 'real food' has drawn criticism from some of his MAHA supporters.
agreements: Measles outbreaks are an ongoing problem in the United States
The US measles situation has become a significant public health and political issue
The timing of measles-related decisions is intersecting with the upcoming US midterm elections

disagreements.topic: Framing of the political dynamics around the measles response

positions.position: Frames the story around Kennedy's political pivot away from vaccines toward 'real food' and the resulting anger and sense of 'betrayal' among his MAHA allies
Frames the story around the delay of the elimination status meeting, highlighting that the timing conveniently pushes the decision past midterm elections, implying political motivations
Frames the story primarily as a straightforward public health matter, focusing on Bhattacharya's pro-vaccine messaging and CDC steps to address outbreaks without emphasizing political maneuvering

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on accountability for the measles crisis

positions.position: Places focus on Kennedy's leadership and his supporters' discontent with his handling of vaccine issues
Centers the CDC and Bhattacharya as proactively addressing measles, presenting a more institutional response-focused narrative

uniqueAngles.angle: Uniquely reports on internal discontent among Kennedy's MAHA movement allies who feel betrayed by his shift in focus from vaccines to 'real food' ahead of midterms
Uniquely focuses on the delay of the international meeting to evaluate US measles elimination status until November, and experts noting this timing places the decision after the midterm elections

Uniquely details Bhattacharya's specific actions as acting CDC director, including his video statement on X urging measles vaccination, and the operational steps the CDC is taking to address outbreaks

stories.headline: Trump tariffs weigh on heavy equipment industry and reshape global trade dynamics

articles.title: Tariffs Force Down Heavy Equipment Sales and Jobs
articles.description: An industry report shows high interest rates, fewer infrastructure projects and tariffs pressures have slowed growth and decreased jobs in the sector.

articles.title: The Unexpected Winners From Trump’s New Global Tariff
articles.description: The president’s flat 10 percent tariff is most beneficial to nations that previously faced the highest rates. But it’s not clear how much that will prompt a new surge in imports.

articles.title: No, Mr. President, Section 122 Tariffs Won’t Work Either
articles.description: Despite Donald Trump’s claims, history shows that Section 122 does not justify his new tariffs.

analysis.summary: Trump's tariff policies are creating ripple effects across the economy, slowing heavy equipment sales and jobs while reshaping global trade dynamics. A flat 10 percent tariff is producing uneven consequences for different nations, and legal and historical questions are being raised about the justification for these tariffs under Section 122.
agreements: Trump's tariffs are having significant effects on trade and the broader economy
The tariff policies are generating controversy and debate over their impact

disagreements.topic: Primary framing of the tariff impact

positions.position: Frames tariffs as one of several factors (alongside high interest rates and fewer infrastructure projects) causing concrete economic harm in the form of lost jobs and slowed growth in the heavy equipment sector

Frames the tariff impact in more nuanced terms, noting there are 'unexpected winners' — nations that previously faced the highest tariff rates — though expresses uncertainty about whether this will boost imports
Frames the issue primarily as a legal and constitutional challenge, arguing that Trump's invocation of Section 122 to justify the tariffs is historically and legally unsound

uniqueAngles.angle: Provides sector-specific economic data, focusing on the heavy equipment industry and the combined effects of tariffs, high interest rates, and reduced infrastructure spending on jobs and growth

Examines which nations may paradoxically benefit from the flat 10 percent tariff structure, particularly those that previously faced higher rates, introducing a winners-and-losers global trade perspective

Focuses on the legal authority question, specifically challenging Trump's use of Section 122 as a justification for tariffs and arguing that historical precedent undermines the administration's legal rationale

stories.headline: Lions trade running back David Montgomery to Texans

articles.title: Lions trade David Montgomery to Texans hours after veteran's sarcastic response to rumors
articles.description: The Lions traded David Montgomery to the Texans in a blockbuster NFL move just hours after the veteran running back slammed a report saying he wanted out.

articles.title: ‘Heartbroken’ Jahmyr Gibbs posts gutting reaction to Lions’ David Montgomery trade
articles.description: Detroit's backfield is going to be missing its "Knuckles" next season.

analysis.summary: The Detroit Lions traded running back David Montgomery to the Houston Texans. The trade came shortly after Montgomery had sarcastically responded to reports that he wanted to leave Detroit, and his backfield partner Jahmyr Gibbs publicly expressed heartbreak over the move.
agreements: David Montgomery was traded from the Lions to the Texans
Montgomery was a veteran running back for the Lions

disagreements.topic: Framing and emphasis of the story

positions.position: Frames the story around the ironic timing of Montgomery's sarcastic denial of trade rumors just hours before the trade was completed
Frames the story around the emotional reaction of teammate Jahmyr Gibbs to losing his backfield partner

uniqueAngles.angle: Highlights Montgomery's sarcastic public response slamming a report that he wanted out of Detroit, noting the trade happened just hours later

Focuses on Jahmyr Gibbs' emotional 'heartbroken' reaction, emphasizing the personal bond between the two running backs and referencing Montgomery's 'Knuckles' nickname from their 'Sonic and Knuckles' backfield pairing

stories.headline: Fox News reaches $787 million settlement with Dominion Voting Systems in defamation case

articles.title: Some on-air claims about Dominion Voting Systems were false, Fox News acknowledges in statement after deal is announced
Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
Here are the 20 specific Fox broadcasts and tweets Dominion says were defamatory
articles.description:
• Fox-Dominion trial delay 'is not unusual,' judge says
• Fox News' defamation battle isn't stopping Trump's election lies

articles.title: Judge in Fox News-Dominion defamation trial: 'The parties have resolved their case'
articles.description: The judge just announced in court that a settlement has been reached in the historic defamation case between Fox News and Dominion Voting Systems.

articles.title: 'Difficult to say with a straight face': Tapper reacts to Fox News' statement on settlement
articles.description: A settlement has been reached in Dominion Voting Systems' defamation case against Fox News, the judge for the case announced. The network will pay more than $787 million to Dominion, a lawyer for the company said.
